senses.gloss: to cut, pierce, or slit, especially like a vulture or another bird of prey
to cut, pierce, or slit, especially like a vulture or another bird of prey; to pluck
to bad-mouth, to malign, to abuse verbally, to slander, to slur
Nasr, one of the of five false gods worshipped by the people of Noah.

examples.text: وَقَالُوا لَا تَذَرُنَّ ءَالِهَتَكُمْ وَلَا تَذَرُنَّ وَدًّا وَلَا سُوَاعًا وَلَا يَغُوثَ وَيَعُوقَ وَنَسْرًا
examples.english: and said (to their people), “Never forsake your gods, and never forsake Wadd, nor Suwā‘, nor Yaghūth and Ya‘ūq and Nasr.”

etymology.text: From Proto-West Semitic *našr-, whence was derived the denominal root ن س ر (n s r).
